has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: aws_cloud_cost__daily_instance_report
metadata.comment: Daily view of usage and cost metrics across your different Amazone Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) instances.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

report.name: report
report.comment: The bucket/report combo this belongs to. Parsed out of `_file`.

usage_date.name: usage_date
usage_date.comment:
The start date and time for the line item in UTC, inclusive. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_start_date` to `usage_start_date` in the staging layer.

billing_period_start_date.name: billing_period_start_date
billing_period_start_date.comment:
The start date of the billing period that is covered by this report, in UTC. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `bill_billing_period_start_date` to `billing_period_start_date` in the staging layer.

billing_period_end_date.name: billing_period_end_date
billing_period_end_date.comment:
The end date of the billing period that is covered by this report, in UTC. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `bill_billing_period_end_date` to `billing_period_end_date` in the staging layer.

usage_account_id.name: usage_account_id
usage_account_id.comment:
The account ID of the account that used this line item. For organizations, this can be either the management account or a member account. You can use this field to track costs or usage by account.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_account_id` to `usage_account_id` in the staging layer.

usage_account_name.name: usage_account_name
usage_account_name.comment:
The name of the account that used this line item. For organizations, this can be either the management account or a member account. You can use this field to track costs or usage by account.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_account_name` to `usage_account_name` in the staging layer.

bill_payer_account_id.name: bill_payer_account_id
bill_payer_account_id.comment: The account ID of the paying account. For an organization in AWS Organizations, this is the account ID of the management account.

bill_payer_account_name.name: bill_payer_account_name
bill_payer_account_name.comment: The account name of the paying account. For an organization in AWS Organizations, this is the name of the management account.

currency_code.name: currency_code
currency_code.comment:
The currency that this line item is shown in. All AWS customers are billed in US dollars by default.

Aliased from `line_item_currency_code` to `currency_code` in the staging layer.

usage_type.name: usage_type
usage_type.comment:
The usage details of the line item. For example, `USW2-BoxUsage:m2.2xlarge` describes an M2 High Memory Double Extra Large instance in the US West (Oregon) Region.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_type` to `usage_type` in the staging layer.

count_products.name: count_products
count_products.comment: Count of distinct products associated with usage on this day.

count_regions.name: count_regions
count_regions.comment: Count of distinct regions associated with usage on this day.

usage_amount.name: usage_amount
usage_amount.comment:
The amount of usage that you incurred during the specified time period. For size-flexible Reserved Instances, use the `reservation_total_reserved_units` column instead.

Certain subscription charges will have a Usage Amount of 0.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_amount` to `usage_amount` in the staging layer.

normalized_usage_amount.name: normalized_usage_amount
normalized_usage_amount.comment:
The amount of usage that you incurred, in normalized units, for size-flexible RIs. This is equal to `line_item_usage_amount` multiplied by `line_item_normalization_factor`.

Aliased from `line_item_normalized_usage_amount` to `normalized_usage_amount` in the staging layer.

total_reserved_units.name: total_reserved_units
total_reserved_units.comment:
This field populates for both `Fee` and `RIFee` line items with distinct values. This should be used instead of `line_item_usage_amount` (renamed to `usage_amount` in staging) for size-flexible Reserved Instances.

- Fee line items: The total number of units reserved, for the total quantity of leases purchased in your subscription for the entire term. This is calculated by multiplying the `NumberOfReservations` with `UnitsPerReservation`.
- RIFee line items (monthly recurring costs): The total number of available units in your subscription, such as the total number of Amazon EC2 hours in a specific RI subscription.

Aliased from `reservation_total_reserved_units` to `total_reserved_units` in the staging layer.

total_blended_cost.name: total_blended_cost
total_blended_cost.comment:
The `blended_rate` multiplied by the `usage_amount`.

`blended_cost` is blank for line items that have a `line_item_type` of `Discount`. Discounts are calculated using only the unblended cost of a member account, aggregated by member account and SKU. As a result, `blended_cost` is not available for discounts.

Aliased from `line_item_blended_cost` to `blended_cost` in the staging layer.

total_unblended_cost.name: total_unblended_cost
total_unblended_cost.comment:
The `unblended_Cost` is the `unblended_rate` multiplied by the `usageAmount`.

Aliased from `line_item_tax_type` to `tax_type` in the staging layer.

total_public_on_demand_cost.name: total_public_on_demand_cost
total_public_on_demand_cost.comment:
The total cost for the line item based on public On-Demand Instance rates. If you have SKUs with multiple On-Demand public costs, the equivalent cost for the highest tier is displayed. For example, services offering free-tiers or tiered pricing.

Aliased from `pricing_public_on_demand_cost` to `public_on_demand_cost` in the staging layer.

unique_key.name: unique_key
unique_key.comment: Surrogate key hashed on all dimensions in this model.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: aws_cloud_cost__daily_overview
metadata.comment: Aggregation of the [Standard](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cur/latest/userguide/dataexports-create-standard.html) Cost & Usage Report (2.0) exported from AWS. Includes commonly analyzed dimensions, all available metrics, and fields added via the `aws_cloud_cost_report_pass_through_columns` variable.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

report.name: report
report.comment: The bucket/report combo this belongs to. Parsed out of `_file`.

usage_start_date.name: usage_start_date
usage_start_date.comment:
The start date and time for the line item in UTC, inclusive. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_start_date` to `usage_start_date` in the staging layer.

usage_end_date.name: usage_end_date
usage_end_date.comment:
The end date and time for the corresponding line item in UTC, exclusive. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_end_date` to `usage_end_date` in the staging layer.

billing_period_start_date.name: billing_period_start_date
billing_period_start_date.comment:
The start date of the billing period that is covered by this report, in UTC. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `bill_billing_period_start_date` to `billing_period_start_date` in the staging layer.

billing_period_end_date.name: billing_period_end_date
billing_period_end_date.comment:
The end date of the billing period that is covered by this report, in UTC. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `bill_billing_period_end_date` to `billing_period_end_date` in the staging layer.

usage_account_id.name: usage_account_id
usage_account_id.comment:
The account ID of the account that used this line item. For organizations, this can be either the management account or a member account. You can use this field to track costs or usage by account.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_account_id` to `usage_account_id` in the staging layer.

usage_account_name.name: usage_account_name
usage_account_name.comment:
The name of the account that used this line item. For organizations, this can be either the management account or a member account. You can use this field to track costs or usage by account.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_account_name` to `usage_account_name` in the staging layer.

bill_payer_account_id.name: bill_payer_account_id
bill_payer_account_id.comment: The account ID of the paying account. For an organization in AWS Organizations, this is the account ID of the management account.

bill_payer_account_name.name: bill_payer_account_name
bill_payer_account_name.comment: The account name of the paying account. For an organization in AWS Organizations, this is the name of the management account.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.comment:
The ID associated with a specific line item. Until the report is final, the field is blank.

Aliased from `bill_invoice_id` to `invoice_id` in the staging layer.

invoicing_entity.name: invoicing_entity
invoicing_entity.comment:
The AWS entity that issues the invoice.

Aliased from `bill_invoicing_entity` to `invoicing_entity` in the staging layer.

billing_entity.name: billing_entity
billing_entity.comment:
Helps you identify whether your invoices or transactions are for AWS Marketplace or for purchases of any other AWS services.

Aliased from `bill_billing_entity` to `billing_entity` in the staging layer.

bill_type.name: bill_type
bill_type.comment:
The type of bill that this report covers. There are three bill types:

- Anniversary: Line items for services that you used during the month.
- Purchase: Line items for upfront service fees.
- Refund: Line items for refunds. These will have negative cost amounts.

Aliased from `bill_bill_type` to `bill_type` in the staging layer.

line_item_type.name: line_item_type
line_item_type.comment:
Type of charge for the line item:

- `BundledDiscount`: Usage-based discount tied to another service’s usage.
- `Credit`: Credit applied to your bill by AWS.
- `Discount`: Discount applied to usage by AWS. Refer to`line_item_line_item_description` for more information.
- `DiscountedUsage`: Usage billed with Reserved Instance (RI) benefits.
- `Fee`: Upfront annual subscription fee paid (e.g., All/Partial Upfront RI).
- `Refund`: Refunded charges. Review the Description column for details.
- `RIFee`: Monthly recurring RI subscription fee (may be $0 for All Upfront; includes reservation metadata).
- `Tax`: Taxes applied (see `line_item_tax_type`).
- `Usage`: On-Demand usage charges.
- `SavingsPlanUpfrontFee`: One-time upfront Savings Plan fee.
- `SavingsPlanRecurringFee`: Recurring Savings Plan charge.
- `SavingsPlanCoveredUsage`: Usage covered by a Savings Plan.
- `SavingsPlanNegation`: Offset for Savings Plan covered usage.

Aliased from `line_item_line_item_type` to `line_item_type` in staging.

tax_type.name: tax_type
tax_type.comment:
The type of tax that AWS applied to this line item. Null if `line_item_line_item_type != 'Tax'`

Aliased from `line_item_tax_type` to `tax_type` in the staging layer.

purchase_option.name: purchase_option
purchase_option.comment:
How you chose to pay for this line item. Valid values are `All Upfront`, `Partial Upfront`, and `No Upfront`.

Aliased from `pricing_purchase_option` to `purchase_option` in the staging layer.

pricing_term.name: pricing_term
pricing_term.comment: Whether your AWS usage is `Reserved`, `On-Demand`, or `Spot`.

product_fee_code.name: product_fee_code
product_fee_code.comment: The code that refers to the fee.

product_fee_description.name: product_fee_description
product_fee_description.comment: The description for the product fee.

pricing_unit.name: pricing_unit
pricing_unit.comment: The pricing unit AWS used to calculate your usage cost. For example, the pricing unit for Amazon EC2 instance usage is in hours.

usage_type.name: usage_type
usage_type.comment:
The usage details of the line item. For example, `USW2-BoxUsage:m2.2xlarge` describes an M2 High Memory Double Extra Large instance in the US West (Oregon) Region.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_type` to `usage_type` in the staging layer.

currency_code.name: currency_code
currency_code.comment:
The currency that this line item is shown in. All AWS customers are billed in US dollars by default.

Aliased from `line_item_currency_code` to `currency_code` in the staging layer.

line_item_description.name: line_item_description
line_item_description.comment:
The description of the line item type. For example, the description of a usage line item summarizes the type of usage incurred during a specific time period.

For size-flexible RIs, the description corresponds to the RI the benefit was applied to. For example, if a line item corresponds to a `t2.micro` and a `t2.small` RI was applied to the usage, the `line_item_line_item_description` displays `t2.small`.

The description for a usage line item with an RI discount contains the pricing plan covered by the line item.

Aliased from `line_item_line_item_description` to `line_item_description` in the staging layer.

product_code.name: product_code
product_code.comment:
The code of the product measured. For example, Amazon EC2 is the product code for Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud.

Aliased from `line_item_product_code` to `product_code` in the staging layer.

product_name.name: product_name
product_name.comment:
The longform name of the product.

Aliased from `product_product_name` to `product_name` in the staging layer.

product_service_code.name: product_service_code
product_service_code.comment:
This identifies the specific AWS service to the customer as a unique short abbreviation.

Aliased from `product_servicecode` to `product_service_code` in the staging layer.

product_family.name: product_family
product_family.comment:
The category for the type of product.

Aliased from `product_product_family` to `product_family` in the staging layer.

operation.name: operation
operation.comment:
The specific AWS operation covered by this line item. This describes the specific usage of the line item. For example, a value of `RunInstances` indicates the operation of an Amazon EC2 instance.

Aliased from `line_item_operation` to `operation` and coalesced with `product_operation` in the staging layer.

instance_type.name: instance_type
instance_type.comment:
Describes the instance type, size, and family, which define the CPU, networking, and storage capacity of your instance.

Aliased from `product_instance_type` to `product_instance` in the staging layer.

instance_family.name: instance_family
instance_family.comment:
Describes your Amazon EC2 instance family. Amazon EC2 provides you with a large number of options across 10 different instance types, each with one or more size options, organized into distinct instance families optimized for different types of applications.

Aliased from `product_instance_family` to `instance_family` in the staging layer.

location.name: location
location.comment:
Describes the Region that your Amazon S3 bucket resides in.

Aliased from `product_location` to `location` in the staging layer.

location_type.name: location_type
location_type.comment:
Describes the endpoint of your task.

Aliased from `product_location_type` to `location_type` in the staging layer.

region_code.name: region_code
region_code.comment:
A Region is a physical location around the world where data centers are clustered. AWS calls each group of logical data centers an Availability Zone (AZ). Each AWS Region consists of multiple, isolated, and physically separate AZs within a geographical area. The Region code attribute has the same name as an AWS Region, and specifies where the AWS service is available.

Aliased from `product_region_code` to `region_code` in the staging layer.

availability_zone.name: availability_zone
availability_zone.comment:
The Availability Zone that hosts this line item. For example, `us-east-1a` or `us-east-1b`. Availability Zones are multiple, isolated locations within each Region.

Aliased from `line_item_availability_zone` to `availability_zone` in the staging layer.

from_location.name: from_location
from_location.comment:
Describes the location where the usage originated from.

Aliased from `product_from_location` to `from_location` in the staging layer.

from_location_type.name: from_location_type
from_location_type.comment:
Describes the location type where the usage originated from.

Aliased from `product_from_location_type` to `from_location_type` in the staging layer.

from_region_code.name: from_region_code
from_region_code.comment:
Describes the source Region code for the AWS service.

Aliased from `product_from_region_code` to `from_region_code` in the staging layer.

to_location.name: to_location
to_location.comment:
Describes the location usage destination.

Aliased from `product_to_location` to `to_location` in the staging layer.

to_location_type.name: to_location_type
to_location_type.comment:
Describes the destination location of the service usage.

Aliased from `product_to_location_type` to `to_location_type` in the staging layer.

to_region_code.name: to_region_code
to_region_code.comment:
Describes the source Region code for the AWS service.

Aliased from `product_to_region_code` to `to_region_code` in the staging layer.

usage_amount.name: usage_amount
usage_amount.comment:
The amount of usage that you incurred during the specified time period. For size-flexible Reserved Instances, use the `reservation_total_reserved_units` column instead.

Certain subscription charges will have a Usage Amount of 0.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_amount` to `usage_amount` in the staging layer.

normalized_usage_amount.name: normalized_usage_amount
normalized_usage_amount.comment:
The amount of usage that you incurred, in normalized units, for size-flexible RIs. This is equal to `line_item_usage_amount` multiplied by `line_item_normalization_factor`.

Aliased from `line_item_normalized_usage_amount` to `normalized_usage_amount` in the staging layer.

normalization_factor.name: normalization_factor
normalization_factor.comment:
As long as the instance has shared tenancy, AWS can apply all Regional Linux or Unix Amazon EC2 and Amazon RDS RI discounts to all instance sizes in an instance family and AWS Region. This also applies to RI discounts for member accounts in an organization. All new and existing Amazon EC2 and Amazon RDS size-flexible RIs are sized according to a normalization factor, based on the instance size.

Aliased from `line_item_normalization_factor` to `normalization_factor` in the staging layer.

blended_cost.name: blended_cost
blended_cost.comment:
The `blended_rate` multiplied by the `usage_amount`.

`blended_cost` is blank for line items that have a `line_item_type` of `Discount`. Discounts are calculated using only the unblended cost of a member account, aggregated by member account and SKU. As a result, `blended_cost` is not available for discounts.

Aliased from `line_item_blended_cost` to `blended_cost` in the staging layer.

unblended_cost.name: unblended_cost
unblended_cost.comment:
The `unblended_Cost` is the `unblended_rate` multiplied by the `usageAmount`.

Aliased from `line_item_tax_type` to `tax_type` in the staging layer.

public_on_demand_cost.name: public_on_demand_cost
public_on_demand_cost.comment:
The total cost for the line item based on public On-Demand Instance rates. If you have SKUs with multiple On-Demand public costs, the equivalent cost for the highest tier is displayed. For example, services offering free-tiers or tiered pricing.

Aliased from `pricing_public_on_demand_cost` to `public_on_demand_cost` in the staging layer.

avg_blended_rate.name: avg_blended_rate
avg_blended_rate.comment:
The `blended_rate` is the average cost incurred for each SKU across an organization.

For example, the Amazon S3 blended rates are the total cost of storage divided by the amount of data stored per month. For accounts with RIs, the blended rates are calculated as the average costs of the RIs and the On-Demand Instances.

Blended rates are calculated at the management account level, and used to allocate costs to each member account. For more information, see Blended Rates and Costs in the AWS Billing User [Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/con-bill-blended-rates.html#Blended_CB).

Aliased from `line_item_blended_rate` to `blended_rate` in the staging layer.

avg_unblended_rate.name: avg_unblended_rate
avg_unblended_rate.comment:
In consolidated billing for accounts using AWS Organizations, the unblended rate is the rate associated with an individual account's service usage.

For Amazon EC2 and Amazon RDS line items that have an RI discount applied to them, the unblended rate is 0.

Aliased from `line_item_unblended_rate` to `unblended_rate` in the staging layer.

avg_public_on_demand_rate.name: avg_public_on_demand_rate
avg_public_on_demand_rate.comment:
The public On-Demand Instance rate in this billing period for the specific line item of usage. If you have SKUs with multiple On-Demand public rates, the equivalent rate for the highest tier is displayed. For example, services offering free-tiers or tiered pricing.

Aliased from `pricing_public_on_demand_rate` to `public_on_demand_rate` in the staging layer.

reservation_amortized_upfront_cost_for_u.name: reservation_amortized_upfront_cost_for_usage
reservation_amortized_upfront_cost_for_u.comment: The initial upfront payment for all upfront RIs and partial upfront RIs amortized for usage time. The value is equal to: `reservation_amortized_upfront_fee_for_billing_period` * The normalized usage amount for `DiscountedUsage` line items / The normalized usage amount for the `RIFee`. Because there are no upfront payments for no upfront RIs, the value for a no upfront RI is 0. We don't provide this value for Dedicated Host reservations at this time. The change will be made in a future update.

reservation_amortized_upfront_fee_for_bi.name: reservation_amortized_upfront_fee_for_billing_period
reservation_amortized_upfront_fee_for_bi.comment: Describes how much of the upfront fee for this reservation is costing you for the billing period. The initial upfront payment for all upfront RIs and partial upfront RIs, amortized over this month. Because there are no upfront fees for no upfront RIs, the value for no upfront RIs is 0. AWS doesn't provide this value for Dedicated Host reservations at this time. They claim tshe change will be made in a future update.

reservation_effective_cost.name: reservation_effective_cost
reservation_effective_cost.comment: The sum of both the upfront and hourly rate of your RI, averaged into an effective hourly rate. `reservation_effective_cost` is calculated by taking the `reservation_amortized_upfront_cost_for_usage` and adding it to the `reservation_recurring_fee_for_usage`.

number_of_reservations.name: number_of_reservations
number_of_reservations.comment:
The number of reservations that are covered by this subscription. For example, one RI subscription might have four associated RI reservations.

Aliased from `reservation_number_of_reservations` to `number_of_reservations` in the staging layer.

normalized_units_per_reservation.name: normalized_units_per_reservation
normalized_units_per_reservation.comment:
The number of normalized units for each instance of a reservation subscription.

Aliased from `reservation_normalized_units_per_reservation` to `normalized_units_per_reservation` in the staging layer.

units_per_reservation.name: units_per_reservation
units_per_reservation.comment:
This field populates for both `Fee` and `RIFee` line items with distinct values.

- Fee line items: The total number of units reserved for the subscription, such as the total number of RI hours purchased for the term of the subscription.
- RIFee line items (monthly recurring costs): The total number of available units in your subscription, such as the total number of Amazon EC2 hours in a specific RI subscription.

Aliased from `reservation_units_per_reservation` to `units_per_reservation` in the staging layer.

total_reserved_normalized_units.name: total_reserved_normalized_units
total_reserved_normalized_units.comment:
The total number of reserved normalized units for all instances for a reservation subscription. AWS computes total normalized units by multiplying the `reservation_normalized_units_per_reservation` with `reservation_number_of_reservations`.

Aliased from `reservation_total_reserved_normalized_units` to `total_reserved_normalized_units` in the staging layer.

total_reserved_units.name: total_reserved_units
total_reserved_units.comment:
This field populates for both `Fee` and `RIFee` line items with distinct values. This should be used instead of `line_item_usage_amount` (renamed to `usage_amount` in staging) for size-flexible Reserved Instances.

- Fee line items: The total number of units reserved, for the total quantity of leases purchased in your subscription for the entire term. This is calculated by multiplying the `NumberOfReservations` with `UnitsPerReservation`.
- RIFee line items (monthly recurring costs): The total number of available units in your subscription, such as the total number of Amazon EC2 hours in a specific RI subscription.

Aliased from `reservation_total_reserved_units` to `total_reserved_units` in the staging layer.

reservation_recurring_fee_for_usage.name: reservation_recurring_fee_for_usage
reservation_recurring_fee_for_usage.comment: The recurring fee amortized for usage time, for partial upfront RIs and no upfront RIs. The value is equal to: The unblended cost of the `RIFee` * The sum of the normalized usage amount of Usage line items / The normalized usage amount of the `RIFee` for size flexible Reserved Instances. Because all upfront RIs don't have recurring fee payments greater than 0, the value for all upfront RIs is 0.

reservation_unused_amortized_upfront_fee.name: reservation_unused_amortized_upfront_fee_for_billing_period
reservation_unused_amortized_upfront_fee.comment: The amortized-upfront-fee-for-billing-period-column amortized portion of the initial upfront fee for all upfront RIs and partial upfront RIs. Because there are no upfront payments for no upfront RIs, the value for no upfront RIs is 0. According to AWS, they doesn't provide this value for Dedicated Host reservations at this time, but the change will be made in a future update.

reservation_unused_normalized_unit_quant.name: reservation_unused_normalized_unit_quantity
reservation_unused_normalized_unit_quant.comment: The number of unused normalized units for a size-flexible Regional RI that you didn't use during this billing period.

reservation_unused_quantity.name: reservation_unused_quantity
reservation_unused_quantity.comment: The number of RI hours that you didn't use during this billing period.

reservation_unused_recurring_fee.name: reservation_unused_recurring_fee
reservation_unused_recurring_fee.comment: The recurring fees associated with your unused reservation hours for partial upfront and no upfront RIs. Because all upfront RIs don't have recurring fees greater than 0, the value for All Upfront RIs is 0.

reservation_upfront_value.name: reservation_upfront_value
reservation_upfront_value.comment: The upfront price paid for your AWS Reserved Instance. For no upfront RIs, this value is 0.

savings_plan_amortized_upfront_commitmen.name: savings_plan_amortized_upfront_commitment_for_billing_period
savings_plan_amortized_upfront_commitmen.comment: The amount of upfront fee a Savings Plan subscription is costing you for the billing period. The initial upfront payment for All Upfront Savings Plan and Partial Upfront Savings Plan amortized over the current month. For No Upfront Savings Plan, the value is 0.

savings_plan_recurring_commitment_for_bi.name: savings_plan_recurring_commitment_for_billing_period
savings_plan_recurring_commitment_for_bi.comment: The monthly recurring fee for your Savings Plan subscriptions. For example, the recurring monthly fee for a Partial Upfront Savings Plan or No Upfront Savings Plan.

savings_plan_effective_cost.name: savings_plan_effective_cost
savings_plan_effective_cost.comment:
The proportion of the Savings Plan monthly commitment amount (upfront and recurring) that is allocated to each usage line.

Aliased from `savings_plan_savings_plan_effective_cost` to `savings_plan_effective_cost` in the staging layer.

savings_plan_rate.name: savings_plan_rate
savings_plan_rate.comment:
The Savings Plan rate for the usage.

Aliased from `savings_plan_savings_plan_rate` to `savings_plan_rate` in the staging layer.

savings_plan_total_commitment_to_date.name: savings_plan_total_commitment_to_date
savings_plan_total_commitment_to_date.comment: The total amortized upfront commitment and recurring commitment to date, for that hour.

savings_plan_used_commitment.name: savings_plan_used_commitment
savings_plan_used_commitment.comment: The total dollar amount of the Savings Plan commitment used. (`Savings_plan_rate` multiplied by usage)

unique_key.name: unique_key
unique_key.comment: Surrogate key hashed on all dimensions in this model.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: aws_cloud_cost__daily_product_report
metadata.comment: Daily view of product usage + accrued costs for each account's billing period.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

report.name: report
report.comment: The bucket/report combo this belongs to. Parsed out of `_file`.

usage_date.name: usage_date
usage_date.comment:
The start date and time for the line item in UTC, inclusive. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_start_date` to `usage_start_date` in the staging layer.

billing_period_start_date.name: billing_period_start_date
billing_period_start_date.comment:
The start date of the billing period that is covered by this report, in UTC. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `bill_billing_period_start_date` to `billing_period_start_date` in the staging layer.

billing_period_end_date.name: billing_period_end_date
billing_period_end_date.comment:
The end date of the billing period that is covered by this report, in UTC. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `bill_billing_period_end_date` to `billing_period_end_date` in the staging layer.

usage_account_id.name: usage_account_id
usage_account_id.comment:
The account ID of the account that used this line item. For organizations, this can be either the management account or a member account. You can use this field to track costs or usage by account.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_account_id` to `usage_account_id` in the staging layer.

usage_account_name.name: usage_account_name
usage_account_name.comment:
The name of the account that used this line item. For organizations, this can be either the management account or a member account. You can use this field to track costs or usage by account.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_account_name` to `usage_account_name` in the staging layer.

bill_payer_account_id.name: bill_payer_account_id
bill_payer_account_id.comment: The account ID of the paying account. For an organization in AWS Organizations, this is the account ID of the management account.

bill_payer_account_name.name: bill_payer_account_name
bill_payer_account_name.comment: The account name of the paying account. For an organization in AWS Organizations, this is the name of the management account.

currency_code.name: currency_code
currency_code.comment:
The currency that this line item is shown in. All AWS customers are billed in US dollars by default.

Aliased from `line_item_currency_code` to `currency_code` in the staging layer.

product_code.name: product_code
product_code.comment:
The code of the product measured. For example, Amazon EC2 is the product code for Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud.

Aliased from `line_item_product_code` to `product_code` in the staging layer.

product_name.name: product_name
product_name.comment:
The longform name of the product.

Aliased from `product_product_name` to `product_name` in the staging layer.

count_regions.name: count_regions
count_regions.comment: Count of distinct regions associated with product usage on this day.

count_instances.name: count_instances
count_instances.comment: Count of distinct instances associated with product usage on this day.

usage_amount.name: usage_amount
usage_amount.comment:
The amount of usage that you incurred during the specified time period. For size-flexible Reserved Instances, use the `reservation_total_reserved_units` column instead.

Certain subscription charges will have a Usage Amount of 0.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_amount` to `usage_amount` in the staging layer.

normalized_usage_amount.name: normalized_usage_amount
normalized_usage_amount.comment:
The amount of usage that you incurred, in normalized units, for size-flexible RIs. This is equal to `line_item_usage_amount` multiplied by `line_item_normalization_factor`.

Aliased from `line_item_normalized_usage_amount` to `normalized_usage_amount` in the staging layer.

total_reserved_units.name: total_reserved_units
total_reserved_units.comment:
This field populates for both `Fee` and `RIFee` line items with distinct values. This should be used instead of `line_item_usage_amount` (renamed to `usage_amount` in staging) for size-flexible Reserved Instances.

- Fee line items: The total number of units reserved, for the total quantity of leases purchased in your subscription for the entire term. This is calculated by multiplying the `NumberOfReservations` with `UnitsPerReservation`.
- RIFee line items (monthly recurring costs): The total number of available units in your subscription, such as the total number of Amazon EC2 hours in a specific RI subscription.

Aliased from `reservation_total_reserved_units` to `total_reserved_units` in the staging layer.

total_blended_cost.name: total_blended_cost
total_blended_cost.comment:
The `blended_rate` multiplied by the `usage_amount`.

`blended_cost` is blank for line items that have a `line_item_type` of `Discount`. Discounts are calculated using only the unblended cost of a member account, aggregated by member account and SKU. As a result, `blended_cost` is not available for discounts.

Aliased from `line_item_blended_cost` to `blended_cost` in the staging layer.

total_unblended_cost.name: total_unblended_cost
total_unblended_cost.comment:
The `unblended_Cost` is the `unblended_rate` multiplied by the `usageAmount`.

Aliased from `line_item_tax_type` to `tax_type` in the staging layer.

total_public_on_demand_cost.name: total_public_on_demand_cost
total_public_on_demand_cost.comment:
The total cost for the line item based on public On-Demand Instance rates. If you have SKUs with multiple On-Demand public costs, the equivalent cost for the highest tier is displayed. For example, services offering free-tiers or tiered pricing.

Aliased from `pricing_public_on_demand_cost` to `public_on_demand_cost` in the staging layer.

unique_key.name: unique_key
unique_key.comment: Surrogate key hashed on all dimensions in this model.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_aws_cloud_cost__report
metadata.comment:
[Standard](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cur/latest/userguide/dataexports-create-standard.html) Cost & Usage Report (2.0) exported from AWS. Majority of columns are prepended with and reflect information from the following categories (others are either system-generated or JSON fields):

- `bill_`: Data about your bill for the billing period
- `identity_`: Data to identify a line item
- `line_item_`: Data about cost, usage, type of usage, pricing rates, product name, and more
- `pricing_`: Data about the pricing for a line item
- `product_`: Data about the product that is being charged in the line item
- `reservation_`: Data about a reservation that applies to the line item
- `savings_plan_`: Data about savings plans that apply to the line item

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be empty.

_file.name: _file
_file.comment: Title of the report file from AWS. If you have selected the "create" report delivery, in which each data export produces a new, timestamped version of each report, the `_file` will include this version timestamp. Otherwise, there will be one version of the file that is repeatedly overwritten/appended to by Amazon.

report.name: report
report.comment: The bucket/report combo this belongs to. Parsed out of `_file`.

_line.name: _line
_line.comment: Line number of the report file this record was read from.

_modified.name: _modified
_modified.comment: Timestamp of when the file was read from AWS.

is_latest_file_version.name: is_latest_file_version
is_latest_file_version.comment: Boolean used to capture the latest export of each billing & usage report.

bill_type.name: bill_type
bill_type.comment:
The type of bill that this report covers. There are three bill types:

- Anniversary: Line items for services that you used during the month.
- Purchase: Line items for upfront service fees.
- Refund: Line items for refunds. These will have negative cost amounts.

Aliased from `bill_bill_type` to `bill_type` in the staging layer.

billing_entity.name: billing_entity
billing_entity.comment:
Helps you identify whether your invoices or transactions are for AWS Marketplace or for purchases of any other AWS services.

Aliased from `bill_billing_entity` to `billing_entity` in the staging layer.

billing_period_start_date.name: billing_period_start_date
billing_period_start_date.comment:
The start date of the billing period that is covered by this report, in UTC. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `bill_billing_period_start_date` to `billing_period_start_date` in the staging layer.

billing_period_end_date.name: billing_period_end_date
billing_period_end_date.comment:
The end date of the billing period that is covered by this report, in UTC. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `bill_billing_period_end_date` to `billing_period_end_date` in the staging layer.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.comment:
The ID associated with a specific line item. Until the report is final, the field is blank.

Aliased from `bill_invoice_id` to `invoice_id` in the staging layer.

invoicing_entity.name: invoicing_entity
invoicing_entity.comment:
The AWS entity that issues the invoice.

Aliased from `bill_invoicing_entity` to `invoicing_entity` in the staging layer.

bill_payer_account_id.name: bill_payer_account_id
bill_payer_account_id.comment: The account ID of the paying account. For an organization in AWS Organizations, this is the account ID of the management account.

bill_payer_account_name.name: bill_payer_account_name
bill_payer_account_name.comment: The account name of the paying account. For an organization in AWS Organizations, this is the name of the management account.

blended_cost.name: blended_cost
blended_cost.comment:
The `blended_rate` multiplied by the `usage_amount`.

`blended_cost` is blank for line items that have a `line_item_type` of `Discount`. Discounts are calculated using only the unblended cost of a member account, aggregated by member account and SKU. As a result, `blended_cost` is not available for discounts.

Aliased from `line_item_blended_cost` to `blended_cost` in the staging layer.

blended_rate.name: blended_rate
blended_rate.comment:
The `blended_rate` is the average cost incurred for each SKU across an organization.

For example, the Amazon S3 blended rates are the total cost of storage divided by the amount of data stored per month. For accounts with RIs, the blended rates are calculated as the average costs of the RIs and the On-Demand Instances.

Blended rates are calculated at the management account level, and used to allocate costs to each member account. For more information, see Blended Rates and Costs in the AWS Billing User [Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awsaccountbilling/latest/aboutv2/con-bill-blended-rates.html#Blended_CB).

Aliased from `line_item_blended_rate` to `blended_rate` in the staging layer.

currency_code.name: currency_code
currency_code.comment:
The currency that this line item is shown in. All AWS customers are billed in US dollars by default.

Aliased from `line_item_currency_code` to `currency_code` in the staging layer.

normalization_factor.name: normalization_factor
normalization_factor.comment:
As long as the instance has shared tenancy, AWS can apply all Regional Linux or Unix Amazon EC2 and Amazon RDS RI discounts to all instance sizes in an instance family and AWS Region. This also applies to RI discounts for member accounts in an organization. All new and existing Amazon EC2 and Amazon RDS size-flexible RIs are sized according to a normalization factor, based on the instance size.

Aliased from `line_item_normalization_factor` to `normalization_factor` in the staging layer.

normalized_usage_amount.name: normalized_usage_amount
normalized_usage_amount.comment:
The amount of usage that you incurred, in normalized units, for size-flexible RIs. This is equal to `line_item_usage_amount` multiplied by `line_item_normalization_factor`.

Aliased from `line_item_normalized_usage_amount` to `normalized_usage_amount` in the staging layer.

availability_zone.name: availability_zone
availability_zone.comment:
The Availability Zone that hosts this line item. For example, `us-east-1a` or `us-east-1b`. Availability Zones are multiple, isolated locations within each Region.

Aliased from `line_item_availability_zone` to `availability_zone` in the staging layer.

line_item_description.name: line_item_description
line_item_description.comment:
The description of the line item type. For example, the description of a usage line item summarizes the type of usage incurred during a specific time period.

For size-flexible RIs, the description corresponds to the RI the benefit was applied to. For example, if a line item corresponds to a `t2.micro` and a `t2.small` RI was applied to the usage, the `line_item_line_item_description` displays `t2.small`.

The description for a usage line item with an RI discount contains the pricing plan covered by the line item.

Aliased from `line_item_line_item_description` to `line_item_description` in the staging layer.

line_item_type.name: line_item_type
line_item_type.comment:
Type of charge for the line item:

- `BundledDiscount`: Usage-based discount tied to another service’s usage.
- `Credit`: Credit applied to your bill by AWS.
- `Discount`: Discount applied to usage by AWS. Refer to`line_item_line_item_description` for more information.
- `DiscountedUsage`: Usage billed with Reserved Instance (RI) benefits.
- `Fee`: Upfront annual subscription fee paid (e.g., All/Partial Upfront RI).
- `Refund`: Refunded charges. Review the Description column for details.
- `RIFee`: Monthly recurring RI subscription fee (may be $0 for All Upfront; includes reservation metadata).
- `Tax`: Taxes applied (see `line_item_tax_type`).
- `Usage`: On-Demand usage charges.
- `SavingsPlanUpfrontFee`: One-time upfront Savings Plan fee.
- `SavingsPlanRecurringFee`: Recurring Savings Plan charge.
- `SavingsPlanCoveredUsage`: Usage covered by a Savings Plan.
- `SavingsPlanNegation`: Offset for Savings Plan covered usage.

Aliased from `line_item_line_item_type` to `line_item_type` in staging.

tax_type.name: tax_type
tax_type.comment:
The type of tax that AWS applied to this line item. Null if `line_item_line_item_type != 'Tax'`

Aliased from `line_item_tax_type` to `tax_type` in the staging layer.

operation.name: operation
operation.comment:
The specific AWS operation covered by this line item. This describes the specific usage of the line item. For example, a value of `RunInstances` indicates the operation of an Amazon EC2 instance.

Aliased from `line_item_operation` to `operation` and coalesced with `product_operation` in the staging layer.

product_code.name: product_code
product_code.comment:
The code of the product measured. For example, Amazon EC2 is the product code for Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud.

Aliased from `line_item_product_code` to `product_code` in the staging layer.

unblended_cost.name: unblended_cost
unblended_cost.comment:
The `unblended_Cost` is the `unblended_rate` multiplied by the `usageAmount`.

Aliased from `line_item_tax_type` to `tax_type` in the staging layer.

unblended_rate.name: unblended_rate
unblended_rate.comment:
In consolidated billing for accounts using AWS Organizations, the unblended rate is the rate associated with an individual account's service usage.

For Amazon EC2 and Amazon RDS line items that have an RI discount applied to them, the unblended rate is 0.

Aliased from `line_item_unblended_rate` to `unblended_rate` in the staging layer.

usage_account_id.name: usage_account_id
usage_account_id.comment:
The account ID of the account that used this line item. For organizations, this can be either the management account or a member account. You can use this field to track costs or usage by account.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_account_id` to `usage_account_id` in the staging layer.

usage_account_name.name: usage_account_name
usage_account_name.comment:
The name of the account that used this line item. For organizations, this can be either the management account or a member account. You can use this field to track costs or usage by account.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_account_name` to `usage_account_name` in the staging layer.

usage_amount.name: usage_amount
usage_amount.comment:
The amount of usage that you incurred during the specified time period. For size-flexible Reserved Instances, use the `reservation_total_reserved_units` column instead.

Certain subscription charges will have a Usage Amount of 0.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_amount` to `usage_amount` in the staging layer.

usage_end_date.name: usage_end_date
usage_end_date.comment:
The end date and time for the corresponding line item in UTC, exclusive. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_end_date` to `usage_end_date` in the staging layer.

usage_start_date.name: usage_start_date
usage_start_date.comment:
The start date and time for the line item in UTC, inclusive. The format is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_start_date` to `usage_start_date` in the staging layer.

usage_type.name: usage_type
usage_type.comment:
The usage details of the line item. For example, `USW2-BoxUsage:m2.2xlarge` describes an M2 High Memory Double Extra Large instance in the US West (Oregon) Region.

Aliased from `line_item_usage_type` to `usage_type` in the staging layer.

public_on_demand_cost.name: public_on_demand_cost
public_on_demand_cost.comment:
The total cost for the line item based on public On-Demand Instance rates. If you have SKUs with multiple On-Demand public costs, the equivalent cost for the highest tier is displayed. For example, services offering free-tiers or tiered pricing.

Aliased from `pricing_public_on_demand_cost` to `public_on_demand_cost` in the staging layer.

public_on_demand_rate.name: public_on_demand_rate
public_on_demand_rate.comment:
The public On-Demand Instance rate in this billing period for the specific line item of usage. If you have SKUs with multiple On-Demand public rates, the equivalent rate for the highest tier is displayed. For example, services offering free-tiers or tiered pricing.

Aliased from `pricing_public_on_demand_rate` to `public_on_demand_rate` in the staging layer.

purchase_option.name: purchase_option
purchase_option.comment:
How you chose to pay for this line item. Valid values are `All Upfront`, `Partial Upfront`, and `No Upfront`.

Aliased from `pricing_purchase_option` to `purchase_option` in the staging layer.

pricing_term.name: pricing_term
pricing_term.comment: Whether your AWS usage is `Reserved`, `On-Demand`, or `Spot`.

pricing_unit.name: pricing_unit
pricing_unit.comment: The pricing unit AWS used to calculate your usage cost. For example, the pricing unit for Amazon EC2 instance usage is in hours.

product_fee_code.name: product_fee_code
product_fee_code.comment: The code that refers to the fee.

product_fee_description.name: product_fee_description
product_fee_description.comment: The description for the product fee.

product_name.name: product_name
product_name.comment:
The longform name of the product.

Aliased from `product_product_name` to `product_name` in the staging layer.

product_family.name: product_family
product_family.comment:
The category for the type of product.

Aliased from `product_product_family` to `product_family` in the staging layer.

product_service_code.name: product_service_code
product_service_code.comment:
This identifies the specific AWS service to the customer as a unique short abbreviation.

Aliased from `product_servicecode` to `product_service_code` in the staging layer.

instance_type.name: instance_type
instance_type.comment:
Describes the instance type, size, and family, which define the CPU, networking, and storage capacity of your instance.

Aliased from `product_instance_type` to `product_instance` in the staging layer.

instance_family.name: instance_family
instance_family.comment:
Describes your Amazon EC2 instance family. Amazon EC2 provides you with a large number of options across 10 different instance types, each with one or more size options, organized into distinct instance families optimized for different types of applications.

Aliased from `product_instance_family` to `instance_family` in the staging layer.

location.name: location
location.comment:
Describes the Region that your Amazon S3 bucket resides in.

Aliased from `product_location` to `location` in the staging layer.

location_type.name: location_type
location_type.comment:
Describes the endpoint of your task.

Aliased from `product_location_type` to `location_type` in the staging layer.

region_code.name: region_code
region_code.comment:
A Region is a physical location around the world where data centers are clustered. AWS calls each group of logical data centers an Availability Zone (AZ). Each AWS Region consists of multiple, isolated, and physically separate AZs within a geographical area. The Region code attribute has the same name as an AWS Region, and specifies where the AWS service is available.

Aliased from `product_region_code` to `region_code` in the staging layer.

from_location.name: from_location
from_location.comment:
Describes the location where the usage originated from.

Aliased from `product_from_location` to `from_location` in the staging layer.

from_location_type.name: from_location_type
from_location_type.comment:
Describes the location type where the usage originated from.

Aliased from `product_from_location_type` to `from_location_type` in the staging layer.

from_region_code.name: from_region_code
from_region_code.comment:
Describes the source Region code for the AWS service.

Aliased from `product_from_region_code` to `from_region_code` in the staging layer.

to_location.name: to_location
to_location.comment:
Describes the location usage destination.

Aliased from `product_to_location` to `to_location` in the staging layer.

to_location_type.name: to_location_type
to_location_type.comment:
Describes the destination location of the service usage.

Aliased from `product_to_location_type` to `to_location_type` in the staging layer.

to_region_code.name: to_region_code
to_region_code.comment:
Describes the source Region code for the AWS service.

Aliased from `product_to_region_code` to `to_region_code` in the staging layer.

reservation_amortized_upfront_cost_for_u.name: reservation_amortized_upfront_cost_for_usage
reservation_amortized_upfront_cost_for_u.comment: The initial upfront payment for all upfront RIs and partial upfront RIs amortized for usage time. The value is equal to: `reservation_amortized_upfront_fee_for_billing_period` * The normalized usage amount for `DiscountedUsage` line items / The normalized usage amount for the `RIFee`. Because there are no upfront payments for no upfront RIs, the value for a no upfront RI is 0. We don't provide this value for Dedicated Host reservations at this time. The change will be made in a future update.

reservation_amortized_upfront_fee_for_bi.name: reservation_amortized_upfront_fee_for_billing_period
reservation_amortized_upfront_fee_for_bi.comment: Describes how much of the upfront fee for this reservation is costing you for the billing period. The initial upfront payment for all upfront RIs and partial upfront RIs, amortized over this month. Because there are no upfront fees for no upfront RIs, the value for no upfront RIs is 0. AWS doesn't provide this value for Dedicated Host reservations at this time. They claim tshe change will be made in a future update.

reservation_effective_cost.name: reservation_effective_cost
reservation_effective_cost.comment: The sum of both the upfront and hourly rate of your RI, averaged into an effective hourly rate. `reservation_effective_cost` is calculated by taking the `reservation_amortized_upfront_cost_for_usage` and adding it to the `reservation_recurring_fee_for_usage`.

number_of_reservations.name: number_of_reservations
number_of_reservations.comment:
The number of reservations that are covered by this subscription. For example, one RI subscription might have four associated RI reservations.

Aliased from `reservation_number_of_reservations` to `number_of_reservations` in the staging layer.

normalized_units_per_reservation.name: normalized_units_per_reservation
normalized_units_per_reservation.comment:
The number of normalized units for each instance of a reservation subscription.

Aliased from `reservation_normalized_units_per_reservation` to `normalized_units_per_reservation` in the staging layer.

units_per_reservation.name: units_per_reservation
units_per_reservation.comment:
This field populates for both `Fee` and `RIFee` line items with distinct values.

- Fee line items: The total number of units reserved for the subscription, such as the total number of RI hours purchased for the term of the subscription.
- RIFee line items (monthly recurring costs): The total number of available units in your subscription, such as the total number of Amazon EC2 hours in a specific RI subscription.

Aliased from `reservation_units_per_reservation` to `units_per_reservation` in the staging layer.

total_reserved_normalized_units.name: total_reserved_normalized_units
total_reserved_normalized_units.comment:
The total number of reserved normalized units for all instances for a reservation subscription. AWS computes total normalized units by multiplying the `reservation_normalized_units_per_reservation` with `reservation_number_of_reservations`.

Aliased from `reservation_total_reserved_normalized_units` to `total_reserved_normalized_units` in the staging layer.

total_reserved_units.name: total_reserved_units
total_reserved_units.comment:
This field populates for both `Fee` and `RIFee` line items with distinct values. This should be used instead of `line_item_usage_amount` (renamed to `usage_amount` in staging) for size-flexible Reserved Instances.

- Fee line items: The total number of units reserved, for the total quantity of leases purchased in your subscription for the entire term. This is calculated by multiplying the `NumberOfReservations` with `UnitsPerReservation`.
- RIFee line items (monthly recurring costs): The total number of available units in your subscription, such as the total number of Amazon EC2 hours in a specific RI subscription.

Aliased from `reservation_total_reserved_units` to `total_reserved_units` in the staging layer.

reservation_recurring_fee_for_usage.name: reservation_recurring_fee_for_usage
reservation_recurring_fee_for_usage.comment: The recurring fee amortized for usage time, for partial upfront RIs and no upfront RIs. The value is equal to: The unblended cost of the `RIFee` * The sum of the normalized usage amount of Usage line items / The normalized usage amount of the `RIFee` for size flexible Reserved Instances. Because all upfront RIs don't have recurring fee payments greater than 0, the value for all upfront RIs is 0.

reservation_unused_amortized_upfront_fee.name: reservation_unused_amortized_upfront_fee_for_billing_period
reservation_unused_amortized_upfront_fee.comment: The amortized-upfront-fee-for-billing-period-column amortized portion of the initial upfront fee for all upfront RIs and partial upfront RIs. Because there are no upfront payments for no upfront RIs, the value for no upfront RIs is 0. According to AWS, they doesn't provide this value for Dedicated Host reservations at this time, but the change will be made in a future update.

reservation_unused_normalized_unit_quant.name: reservation_unused_normalized_unit_quantity
reservation_unused_normalized_unit_quant.comment: The number of unused normalized units for a size-flexible Regional RI that you didn't use during this billing period.

reservation_unused_quantity.name: reservation_unused_quantity
reservation_unused_quantity.comment: The number of RI hours that you didn't use during this billing period.

reservation_unused_recurring_fee.name: reservation_unused_recurring_fee
reservation_unused_recurring_fee.comment: The recurring fees associated with your unused reservation hours for partial upfront and no upfront RIs. Because all upfront RIs don't have recurring fees greater than 0, the value for All Upfront RIs is 0.

reservation_upfront_value.name: reservation_upfront_value
reservation_upfront_value.comment: The upfront price paid for your AWS Reserved Instance. For no upfront RIs, this value is 0.

savings_plan_amortized_upfront_commitmen.name: savings_plan_amortized_upfront_commitment_for_billing_period
savings_plan_amortized_upfront_commitmen.comment: The amount of upfront fee a Savings Plan subscription is costing you for the billing period. The initial upfront payment for All Upfront Savings Plan and Partial Upfront Savings Plan amortized over the current month. For No Upfront Savings Plan, the value is 0.

savings_plan_recurring_commitment_for_bi.name: savings_plan_recurring_commitment_for_billing_period
savings_plan_recurring_commitment_for_bi.comment: The monthly recurring fee for your Savings Plan subscriptions. For example, the recurring monthly fee for a Partial Upfront Savings Plan or No Upfront Savings Plan.

savings_plan_effective_cost.name: savings_plan_effective_cost
savings_plan_effective_cost.comment:
The proportion of the Savings Plan monthly commitment amount (upfront and recurring) that is allocated to each usage line.

Aliased from `savings_plan_savings_plan_effective_cost` to `savings_plan_effective_cost` in the staging layer.

savings_plan_rate.name: savings_plan_rate
savings_plan_rate.comment:
The Savings Plan rate for the usage.

Aliased from `savings_plan_savings_plan_rate` to `savings_plan_rate` in the staging layer.

savings_plan_total_commitment_to_date.name: savings_plan_total_commitment_to_date
savings_plan_total_commitment_to_date.comment: The total amortized upfront commitment and recurring commitment to date, for that hour.

savings_plan_used_commitment.name: savings_plan_used_commitment
savings_plan_used_commitment.comment: The total dollar amount of the Savings Plan commitment used. (`Savings_plan_rate` multiplied by usage)

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table
